title: salix starkeana var. cinerascens
intro: Salix starkeana var. cinerascens, also known as Cinerascens Willow, is a variety of Salix starkeana, a type of willow tree native to the Himalayas. The Salix starkeana var. cinerascens has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, as well as its potential to lower fever and treat various respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Salix starkeana var. cinerascens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
